And then, I will visualize the map for the region data of Chinese subjects Combine all region data and load color data

## combine region data
Journal_region_experi2 <- Journal_region_experi %>% 
  dplyr::mutate(`experimental study in the Journal` = case_when(Count_Bins == "<1" ~ "1",
                                  Count_Bins == "1~5" ~ "2",
                                  Count_Bins == "5~10" ~ "3",
                                  Count_Bins == ">=10" ~ "4")) %>% 
  dplyr::select(Region,`experimental study in the Journal`)


Journal_region_questionaire2 <- Journal_region_questionaire %>% 
  dplyr::mutate(`questionnaire study in the Journal` = case_when(Count_Bins == "<1" ~ "1",
                                  Count_Bins == "1~5" ~ "2",
                                  Count_Bins == "5~10" ~ "3",
                                  Count_Bins == ">=10" ~ "4")) %>% 
  dplyr::select(Region,`questionnaire study in the Journal`)


SupplH1_studytype_map <- H1_Journal_region_map %>% 
  dplyr::left_join(Journal_region_experi2, by = "Region") %>% 
  dplyr::left_join(Journal_region_questionaire2, by = "Region") %>% 
  dplyr::mutate(across(4:5,~ifelse(is.na(.),0,.))) %>% 
  dplyr::rename(Journal = Color)

Finally, I visualized geographic distribution and calculated Bayesian multinomial test for region.

## Journal
SupplH2_Fig_region_general <- ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = China_province_region, fill = NA, color = "gray60", linewidth = 0.3) +
  geom_sf(data = China_line, fill = NA, color = "gray60", linewidth = 0.3) +
  geom_sf(data = SupplH1_studytype_map,aes(fill = factor(`Journal`)))+
  coord_sf(crs = aeqd_crs, xlim = c(-2700, 2400),
            ylim = c(-3700, 2400), 
            expand = FALSE) + 
  scale_fill_manual(values = my_blues,
                    breaks = c("0","1","2", "3", "4"),
                    labels = c("0", "<1%","<5%", "<10%",">=10%"),
                    name = "Proportion",
                    drop = FALSE)+  
  theme_bw()+
  ggtitle("Journal")+
  theme(panel.border = element_blank(),
        panel.grid = element_blank(),
        axis.text = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks = element_blank(),
        plot.title = element_text(color = "black",size = 16,
                                  face = "bold",vjust = 0.1, hjust = 0.5),
        legend.position = "none")


## Journal experi
SupplH2_Fig_Journal_experi_region <- ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = China_province_region, fill = NA, color = "gray60", linewidth = 0.3) +
  geom_sf(data = China_line, fill = NA, color = "gray60", linewidth = 0.3) +
  geom_sf(data = SupplH1_studytype_map,aes(fill = factor(`experimental study in the Journal`)))+
  coord_sf(crs = aeqd_crs, xlim = c(-2700, 2400),
            ylim = c(-3700, 2400), 
            expand = FALSE) + 
  scale_fill_manual(values = my_blues,
                    breaks = c("0","1","2", "3", "4"),
                    labels = c("0", "<1%","<5%", "<10%",">=10%"),
                    name = "Proportion",
                    drop = FALSE)+  
  theme_bw()+
  ggtitle("experimental study  \nin the Journal")+
  theme(panel.border = element_blank(),
        panel.grid = element_blank(),
        axis.text = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks = element_blank(),
        plot.title = element_text(color = "black",size = 16,
                                  face = "bold",vjust = 0.1, hjust = 0.5),
        legend.position = "bottom")


## questionaire
SupplH2_Fig_Journal_questionaire_region <- ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = China_province_region, fill = NA, color = "gray60", linewidth = 0.3) +
  geom_sf(data = China_line, fill = NA, color = "gray60", linewidth = 0.3) +
  geom_sf(data = SupplH1_studytype_map,aes(fill = factor(`questionnaire study in the Journal`)))+
  coord_sf(crs = aeqd_crs, xlim = c(-2700, 2400),
            ylim = c(-3700, 2400), 
            expand = FALSE) + 
  scale_fill_manual(values = my_blues,
                    breaks = c("0","1","2", "3", "4"),
                    labels = c("0", "<1%","<5%", "<10%",">=10%"),
                    name = "Proportion",
                    drop = FALSE)+  
  theme_bw()+
  ggtitle("questionnaire study  \nin the Journal")+
  theme(panel.border = element_blank(),
        panel.grid = element_blank(),
        axis.text = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks = element_blank(),
        plot.title = element_text(color = "black",size = 16,
                                  face = "bold",vjust = 0.1, hjust = 0.5),
        legend.position = "none")


SupplH2_Fig_region_studytype <- SupplH2_Fig_region_general + SupplH2_Fig_Journal_experi_region + SupplH2_Fig_Journal_questionaire_region +
  plot_layout(ncol = 3, nrow = 1, heights = c(2,2))+
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = 'A',
                  theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 16))) + 
  theme(plot.tag = element_text(size = 16))


ggsave(here("3_Data_Analysis","3_2_image","SupplH2_Fig_region_studytype.pdf"), SupplH2_Fig_region_studytype, device = "pdf", width=10, height = 6)

ggsave(here("3_Data_Analysis","3_2_image","SupplH2_Fig_region_studytype.png"), SupplH2_Fig_region_studytype, device = "png", width=10, height = 6, dpi = 600)

SupplH2_Fig_region_studytype

We also try to calculate the Spearman’s correlation between Journal region and SHANGHAI RANKING

## psy2021
ShanghaiRanking_psy2021_provinceRank <- ShanghaiRanking_psy2021 %>% 
  dplyr::rename(Region = 4) %>% 
  dplyr::group_by(Region) %>% 
  dplyr::slice(1) %>% 
  dplyr::ungroup() %>% 
  dplyr::arrange(`排名`) %>% 
  dplyr::mutate(psy2021 = 1:21) %>%    ## give a rank num
  dplyr::select(Region,psy2021)

## applied psy
ShanghaiRanking_appliedpsy2021_provinceRank <- ShanghaiRanking_appliedpsy2021 %>% 
  dplyr::rename(Region = 4) %>% 
  dplyr::group_by(Region) %>% 
  dplyr::slice(1) %>% 
  dplyr::ungroup() %>% 
  dplyr::arrange(`排名`) %>% 
  dplyr::mutate(Appliedpsy2021 = 1:29) %>% 
  dplyr::select(Region,Appliedpsy2021)

## Journal region
Journal_region_ShanghaiRanking <- Journal_region_general_tab2 %>% 
  dplyr::arrange(desc(n)) %>% 
  dplyr::mutate(Journal = 1:27) %>% 
  dplyr::select(Region,Journal)


## create province name data
China_province <- data.frame(Region = c("新疆","西藏","甘肃","青海","内蒙古",
                    "宁夏","四川","重庆","陕西","贵州","云南",
                    "北京","山西","河北","河南","湖北","湖南",
                    "广西","广东","香港","澳门","台湾","海南",
                    "黑龙江","吉林","辽宁","天津","山东","江苏",
                    "安徽","上海","浙江","江西","福建"))

## psy merge data

Region_psy_cordata <- left_join(China_province,ShanghaiRanking_psy2021_provinceRank,by = "Region") %>% 
  left_join(Journal_region_ShanghaiRanking,by = "Region")


## applied psy merge data
Region_appliedpsy_cordata <- left_join(China_province,ShanghaiRanking_appliedpsy2021_provinceRank,by = "Region") %>%
  left_join(Journal_region_ShanghaiRanking,by = "Region")

spearman relation

## psy
BF_psy <- Region_psy_cordata %>% 
correlation(select = "psy2021",
            select2 = "Journal",
            method = "spearman",
            bayesian = TRUE)

BF_psy
## # Correlation Matrix (spearman-method)
## 
## Parameter1 | Parameter2 |  rho |        95% CI |     pd | % in ROPE
## -------------------------------------------------------------------
## psy2021    |    Journal | 0.35 | [-0.01, 0.69] | 95.53% |     9.95%
## 
## Parameter1 |         Prior |   BF
## ---------------------------------
## psy2021    | Beta (3 +- 3) | 2.13
## 
## Observations: 20
log(BF_psy$BF)
## [1] 0.7538843
## applied psy
BF_appliedpsy <- Region_appliedpsy_cordata %>% 
  correlation(select = "Appliedpsy2021",
              select2 = "Journal",
              method = "spearman",
              bayesian =TRUE)

BF_appliedpsy
## # Correlation Matrix (spearman-method)
## 
## Parameter1     | Parameter2 |  rho |       95% CI |      pd | % in ROPE
## -----------------------------------------------------------------------
## Appliedpsy2021 |    Journal | 0.49 | [0.18, 0.73] | 100%*** |     0.80%
## 
## Parameter1     |         Prior |      BF
## ----------------------------------------
## Appliedpsy2021 | Beta (3 +- 3) | 19.79**
## 
## Observations: 25
log(BF_appliedpsy$BF)
## [1] 2.985203

Bibliometric Analysis

BTS data

## split keyword
BTS_keywords_split_rows <- BTS_keywords %>% 
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(Article_Keywords)) %>% 
  tidyr::separate_rows(Article_Keywords,sep = ";") %>% 
  dplyr::mutate(Article_Keywords =  str_to_title(Article_Keywords),
                Article_Keywords = ifelse(Article_Keywords == "Covid-19", "COVID-19",
                                          Article_Keywords))

                
## tab
BTS_keywords_tab <- BTS_keywords_split_rows %>% 
  dplyr::group_by(Article_Keywords) %>% 
  dplyr::summarise(n = n()) %>% 
  dplyr::mutate(cate = factor(n))

BTS wordcloud

Suppl_Fig_BTS_wordcloud <- BTS_keywords_tab %>% 
  ggplot(aes(label = Article_Keywords, 
             size = n)) +
  geom_text_wordcloud(shape = "square", 
                      alpha = 0.8,
                      color = "#56B4E9") +
  scale_size_area(max_size = 8) +
  theme_void()


Suppl_Fig_BTS_wordcloud

Journal data

Journal_keywords2 <- Journal_keywords %>% 
  dplyr::select(Article_IDs,Article_Title,`Keyword-关键词`,`Year-年`) %>% 
  dplyr::rename(Keywords = 3 , Year = 4) %>% 
  tidyr::separate_rows(3, sep = "[;,.\\s|]+") %>% 
  group_by(Keywords) %>%
  summarise(tab = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(tab))


## filter 69
## total
Journal_keywords_tab69 <- Journal_keywords2 %>% 
  arrange(desc(tab)) %>% 
  select(Keywords,tab) %>% 
  slice(1:84)

Journal wordcloud

## save data and convert Chinese keywords into English keywords

# writexl::write_xlsx(Journal_keywords3,"Journal_keywords3.xlsx")
# "Journal_keywords3" and "Journal_keywords_translation" are the same file; the latter is the translated version.

Journal_keywords4 <-  Journal_keywords_translation %>% 
  dplyr::group_by(en) %>% 
  dplyr::mutate(tab = sum(tab),
                cate = factor(tab)) %>%
  dplyr::slice(1) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(desc(tab))


## plot
Suppl_Fig_Journal_wordcloud <- Journal_keywords4 %>% 
  ggplot(aes(label = en, 
             size = tab, 
             color = cate)) +
  geom_text_wordcloud(shape = "square", 
                      alpha = 0.8,
                      color = "#56B4E9") +
  scale_size_area(max_size = 8) +
  theme_void()

Suppl_Fig_Journal_wordcloud 

Suppl_Fig_Wordcloud <- Suppl_Fig_BTS_wordcloud + Suppl_Fig_Journal_wordcloud

Suppl_Fig_Wordcloud
## Warning in wordcloud_boxes(data_points = points_valid_first, boxes = boxes, :
## Some words could not fit on page. They have been placed at their original
## positions.

ggsave(here("3_Data_Analysis","3_2_image","Suppl_Fig_Wordcloud.pdf")
,Suppl_Fig_Wordcloud, device = "pdf", width=18, height = 9)

ggsave(here("3_Data_Analysis","3_2_image","Suppl_Fig_Wordcloud.png")
,Suppl_Fig_Wordcloud, device = "png", width=18, height = 9)

H3 exloration analysis

I continued to use the same steps to divide age into four age group.

Sim_Sens_mult_age_four <- function(Mean1, diff_age, SD1, sample_N=Sample_Size){
  
  
  ageData_age_four <-  c(pnorm(17, mean = Mean1, sd = SD1) * sample_N,
                          (pnorm(40, mean = Mean1, sd = SD1) - pnorm(17, mean = Mean1, sd = SD1)) *sample_N,
                          (pnorm(60, mean = Mean1, sd = SD1) - pnorm(40, mean = Mean1, sd = SD1)) *sample_N,
                          (1 - pnorm(60, mean = Mean1, sd = SD1)) * sample_N)
  
    
  # create a dataframe with ageData_Fourage
  Sim_age_four_df <- setNames(data.frame(matrix(ncol = 2, nrow = 4)), c("ageBins", "sim1"))
  Sim_age_four_df$ageBins <- c("<=17", "18~40", "41~60",">=61")
  Sim_age_four_df$sim1[1:4] <- ageData_age_four
  

  return(Sim_age_four_df)
}

process and transform Journal data

Journal_age_four_general <- Journal_age2 %>% 
  dplyr::filter(Target_Population_N != 1)


Sim_Journal_age_four_general <- lapply(1:nrow(Journal_age_four_general),function(i){
  
  Sim_Sens_mult_age_four(Mean1 = Journal_age_four_general$M_age[i],
                diff_age = 0,
                SD1 = Journal_age_four_general$SD_age[i],
                sample_N = Journal_age_four_general$Sample_Size[i])
  
  })


Sim_Journal_age_four2_general <- do.call(rbind,Sim_Journal_age_four_general)


Sim_Journal_age_four3_general <- Sim_Journal_age_four2_general %>% 
  dplyr::group_by(ageBins) %>% 
  dplyr::summarise(sim1 = sum(sim1,na.rm = TRUE)) %>% 
  dplyr::ungroup() %>% 
  dplyr::mutate(ageBins =factor(ageBins,
                                levels = c("<=17","18~40", "41~60", ">=61")))


## Calculate the sample difference between the coded data and the simulated data
sum(Journal_age_four_general$Sample_Size)-sum(Sim_Journal_age_four3_general$sim1,na.rm = TRUE)
## [1] 0

process and transform BTS data

BTS_age_four_list <- list()

for(i in seq_along(BTS)){
  
  BTS_name <- names(BTS)[i]
  
  if("Age" %in% names(BTS[[i]])){
    
    BTS_age <- BTS[[i]] %>% 
      dplyr::filter(!is.na(Age) & !is.na(ISO3)) %>% 
      dplyr::mutate(Age = as.numeric(Age)) %>% 
      dplyr::select(Article_Id,Age,Target_Population,ISO3) %>% 
      #dplyr::group_by(ISO3) %>% 
      dplyr::mutate(ageBins = cut(Age,
                                breaks = c(0, 17.5, 40.5, 60.5, Inf),
                                labels = c("<=17","18~40", "41~60", ">=61"),
                                levels = c("<=17","18~40","41~60", ">=61")))
  
    
BTS_age_four_list[[BTS_name]] <- BTS_age
  
} else {  
 
    cat(sprintf("Skipping '%s' because it does not contain a 'Age' column.\n", BTS_name))  
  } 
}
## Skipping 'BTS_Atari_et_al_2020' because it does not contain a 'Age' column.
## merge data.frame in list
BTS_age_four <-  do.call(rbind,BTS_age_four_list)

combine data and trans data form for plot

## filter > 30
BTS_age_four_allcountry_filter <-  BTS_age_four %>% 
  dplyr::filter(Article_Id != "Lucca_et_al_2024") %>%  ## filter target population is a and 3
  dplyr::group_by(ISO3) %>% 
  dplyr::summarise(n=n()) %>% 
  dplyr::filter(n > 30) %>% 
  dplyr::ungroup() %>% 
  dplyr::pull(ISO3)


BTS_age_four_allcountry <- BTS_age_four %>% 
  dplyr::filter(ISO3 %in% BTS_age_four_allcountry_filter) %>% 
  dplyr::filter(ISO3 != "CHN") %>% ## drop Chinese data
  dplyr::group_by(ISO3,ageBins) %>% 
  dplyr::summarise(n=n()) %>% 
  dplyr::mutate(Proportion = n/sum(n)*100) %>% 
  dplyr::ungroup()
## `summarise()` has regrouped the output.
## ℹ Summaries were computed grouped by ISO3 and ageBins.
## ℹ Output is grouped by ISO3.
## ℹ Use `summarise(.groups = "drop_last")` to silence this message.
## ℹ Use `summarise(.by = c(ISO3, ageBins))` for per-operation grouping
##   (`?dplyr::dplyr_by`) instead.
## calculate total sum
BTS_age_four_allcountry_sum_prop <- BTS_age_four_allcountry %>% 
  dplyr::group_by(ISO3) %>% 
  dplyr::summarise(sum = sum(Proportion)) %>% 
  dplyr::ungroup()


## Add Chinese data
BTS_age_four_CHN <- BTS_age_four %>% 
  dplyr::filter(Article_Id != "Lucca_et_al_2024") %>% 
  dplyr::filter(ISO3 == "CHN") %>% 
  dplyr::group_by(ISO3,ageBins) %>% 
  dplyr::summarise(n = n()) %>% 
  dplyr::ungroup()
## `summarise()` has regrouped the output.
## ℹ Summaries were computed grouped by ISO3 and ageBins.
## ℹ Output is grouped by ISO3.
## ℹ Use `summarise(.groups = "drop_last")` to silence this message.
## ℹ Use `summarise(.by = c(ISO3, ageBins))` for per-operation grouping
##   (`?dplyr::dplyr_by`) instead.
## combine CHN data
Journal_age_four <- Sim_Journal_age_four3_general %>% 
  dplyr::mutate(ISO3 = "CHN") %>% 
  dplyr::rename(n =sim1)

  
Journal_BTS_age_four_CHN <- bind_rows(BTS_age_four_CHN, Journal_age_four)


Journal_BTS_age_four_CHN2 <- Journal_BTS_age_four_CHN %>% 
  dplyr::group_by(ageBins) %>% 
  dplyr::summarise(n = sum(n)) %>% 
  dplyr::ungroup() %>% 
  dplyr::mutate(ISO3 = "CHN") %>% 
  dplyr::mutate(Proportion = n/sum(n)*100)
 
sum(Journal_BTS_age_four_CHN2$Proportion) 
## [1] 100
## Combine data
H3_age_four <- dplyr::bind_rows(BTS_age_four_allcountry, Journal_BTS_age_four_CHN2)


# table(H3_PsyStages$ageBins)

## Set factor
H3_age_four_factor <- H3_age_four %>%
  dplyr::filter(ageBins == "18~40") %>%
  dplyr::arrange(Proportion) %>%
  dplyr::pull(ISO3)



## Process WEIRD
H3_age_four2 <- H3_age_four %>%
  dplyr::left_join(WEIRD, by = c("ISO3" = "iso3c")) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(WEIRD = ifelse(ISO3 == "XKX","WEIRD",WEIRD)) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(ISO3 = factor(ISO3, levels = H3_age_four_factor)) %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(ageBins))


## 18~40 age 
H3_age_four2 %>% 
  dplyr::distinct(ISO3)
## # A tibble: 128 × 1
##    ISO3 
##    <fct>
##  1 AGO  
##  2 ALB  
##  3 ARE  
##  4 ARG  
##  5 ARM  
##  6 ATG  
##  7 AUS  
##  8 AUT  
##  9 AZE  
## 10 BEL  
## # ℹ 118 more rows
H3_age_four2_1840 <- H3_age_four2 %>% 
  dplyr::filter(ageBins == "18~40") %>% 
  dplyr::filter(Proportion > 60)   ## 124

plot

SupplH3_Fig_age_four <- ggplot(H3_age_four2, aes(x=Proportion, y=ISO3, fill=ageBins)) +
  geom_col(alpha = .75)+
  labs(y="Countries/Regions",fill = "Age bins") + 
  scale_fill_discrete(label=c("0~17", "18~40", "41~60",">=61")) +
  theme_classic()+
  #guides(y="axis_nested") +
  theme(legend.title = element_text(size = 15,family = "serif"),
        axis.title= element_text(size = 15,family = "serif"),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 10,family = "serif"),
        strip.text = element_text(size = 15,family = "serif"),
        strip.background = element_rect(fill = NA,color = NA),
        panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA,color = "black")) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 100, by = 10)) +
  facet_wrap(~ WEIRD, scales = "free",
             labeller = as_labeller(c("Non_WEIRD" = "Non-WEIRD", "WEIRD" = "WEIRD"))) +
  scale_fill_okabe_ito()
## Scale for fill is already present.
## Adding another scale for fill, which will replace the existing scale.
SupplH3_Fig_age_four

ggsave(here("3_Data_Analysis","3_2_image","SupplH3_Fig_age_four.pdf"),
       SupplH3_Fig_age_four,device = "pdf", width=18, height = 18)

ggsave(here("3_Data_Analysis","3_2_image","SupplH3_Fig_age_four.png"),
       SupplH3_Fig_age_four,device = "png", width=18, height = 18, dpi = 600)
